name: TA-65 post-MI pilot trial improved some immune biomarkers but missed its primary immunosenescence endpoint
summary: A more recent human RCT gives mixed biomarker evidence for TA-65 in older adults after myocardial infarction.
statement: In older post-MI patients, TA-65 did not change the predefined CD8+ TEMRA primary endpoint but increased major lymphocyte subsets and lowered hsCRP after 12 months.
population_or_model: Adults over 65 years old after myocardial infarction
quantitative_note: The abstract reports a +285 cells/uL estimated treatment effect for total lymphocytes and 62% lower hsCRP in TA-65 versus placebo at 12 months.
caveats: The primary immunosenescence endpoint was null.
The trial was a pilot study in a post-MI population, not a general aging intervention trial.
The source reports immune and inflammation biomarkers, not telomere-length restoration as a clinical outcome.
